proposed_gaps.title: EFFECTIVE: Migrate roadmap tasks to bd issue tracker

source_of_evidence.section: AGENTS.md mandates `bd` for ALL issue tracking, but docs/ROADMAP.md contains PENDING tasks in markdown format.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: AGENTS.md mandates `bd` for ALL issue tracking, but docs/ROADMAP.md contains PENDING tasks in markdown format.

acceptance_criteria_draft: All 'PENDING' tasks from docs/ROADMAP.md are created as issues in `bd` with appropriate types and priorities.
The docs/ROADMAP.md 'Delegated Tasks' section is updated to reflect that tasks are now tracked in `bd`.
Running `bd ready` shows the newly migrated tasks as available work items.

proposed_gaps.title: INFRA: Complete and merge CI/CD setup

source_of_evidence.section: README.md states 'CI/CD setup in progress', and there is an open PR 'ci: add GitHub Actions workflow'.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: README.md states 'CI/CD setup in progress', and there is an open PR 'ci: add GitHub Actions workflow'.

acceptance_criteria_draft: The 'chump/add-ci' PR is merged into the main branch.
CI pipeline successfully runs lint, test, and build steps on every push and pull request.
README.md is updated to reflect that CI/CD is fully established.

proposed_gaps.title: EFFECTIVE: Implement comprehensive test suite
acceptance_criteria_draft: Core functionalities (e.g., model selection, code generation, quality prediction) have unit and integration tests.
Test coverage metrics are established and meet a minimum threshold (e.g., 70%).
Tests are integrated into the CI pipeline and pass consistently.

proposed_gaps.title: EFFECTIVE: Implement user profiles table with admin/owner roles

source_of_evidence.section: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Create user profiles table with admin/owner roles' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Create user profiles table with admin/owner roles' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task.

acceptance_criteria_draft: A database table for user profiles is created, including fields for user ID, name, email, and role (admin/owner/user).
API endpoints exist to create, read, update, and delete user profiles, respecting role-based access control.
New users are assigned a default role upon registration.

proposed_gaps.title: EFFECTIVE: Build admin dashboard page

source_of_evidence.section: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Build admin dashboard page' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Build admin dashboard page' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task.

acceptance_criteria_draft: A dedicated web page for administrators is accessible only to users with 'admin' or 'owner' roles.
The dashboard displays key system metrics or an overview of user activity (e.g., number of users, active models).
Navigation elements are present to access other admin functionalities (e.g., user management, settings).

proposed_gaps.title: EFFECTIVE: Build admin authentication middleware

source_of_evidence.section: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Build admin authentication middleware' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Build admin authentication middleware' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task.

acceptance_criteria_draft: A middleware layer is implemented to protect admin-specific routes and API endpoints.
Only authenticated users with 'admin' or 'owner' roles can access protected resources.
Unauthorized access attempts are logged and return appropriate error responses (e.g., 403 Forbidden).

proposed_gaps.title: EFFECTIVE: Fix OAuth login password issue

source_of_evidence.section: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Fix OAuth login password issue' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task, noting 'OAuth users don't have password for beast-mode login'.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Fix OAuth login password issue' as a high-priority, ready-to-delegate task, noting 'OAuth users don't have password for beast-mode login'.

acceptance_criteria_draft: OAuth users can successfully log in to BEAST-MODE without needing a separate password.
The system correctly handles user accounts created via OAuth, allowing them to access their profiles and features.
Existing OAuth users are not prompted to set a password for direct BEAST-MODE login.

proposed_gaps.title: DOC: Document and automate session completion workflow

source_of_evidence.section: AGENTS.md states 'When ending a work session, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push… [truncated]' indicating an incomplete but critical workflow.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: AGENTS.md states 'When ending a work session, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push… [truncated]' indicating an incomplete but critical workflow.

acceptance_criteria_draft: A complete, step-by-step guide for agents to end a work session is documented in AGENTS.md or a linked file.
The documented workflow includes `bd` updates, `git` operations, and any other necessary cleanup or reporting.
Consideration is given to automating parts of the session completion process via a script or tool.

proposed_gaps.title: RESILIENT: Verify bd system operational health and visibility

source_of_evidence.section: AGENTS.md mandates `bd` for ALL issue tracking, yet the 'Open issues' signal is empty, suggesting a potential disconnect or lack of visibility.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: AGENTS.md mandates `bd` for ALL issue tracking, yet the 'Open issues' signal is empty, suggesting a potential disconnect or lack of visibility.

acceptance_criteria_draft: Confirm that `bd` issues are correctly persisted to `.beads/issues.jsonl` and are version controlled.
Ensure that `bd ready --json` correctly outputs issues that are unblocked and ready for work.
Investigate why `bd` issues are not reflected in the 'Open issues' signal provided by the external system, or integrate `bd` output with the external view.

proposed_gaps.title: DOC: Update documentation with correct port numbers

source_of_evidence.section: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Update documentation with correct port numbers' as a medium-priority, ready-to-delegate task.
source_of_evidence.excerpt: docs/ROADMAP.md lists 'Update documentation with correct port numbers' as a medium-priority, ready-to-delegate task.

acceptance_criteria_draft: All relevant documentation files (e.g., README.md, installation guides, API docs) are reviewed for port number references.
Any incorrect or outdated port numbers are updated to reflect the current system configuration.
A quick start guide or installation instructions clearly specify the correct port numbers for local development and deployment.
